title: Design the IR for analysis and rewriting
description: Choose an IR (often SSA) whose invariants make data-flow analysis and transformation simple and verifiable, and enforce them.
body: Use when designing or extending the intermediate representation. Do not pick a representation that makes def-use chains or control flow ambiguous, and do not let passes leave the IR in a malformed state. Actions: prefer SSA so each value has a single definition, make control flow explicit as a CFG, define the IR invariants (typed values, dominance, well-formed phi nodes), and run an IR verifier after every pass. Validation signals include the verifier accepting the IR after each transform and analyses producing stable results. Risk boundary: an IR invariant silently broken by one pass causes a later pass to miscompile; treat verifier failures as blocking and require every new pass to leave the IR valid.
